task: Built and deployed a four-stage permission gate system to prevent dangerous task injection in unrestricted sessions, added task approval workflow with notification integration, completed Phase 3.5 security hardening with full test coverage, cut first tagged release (v0.1.0) with Homebrew formula and GitHub release, verified end-to-end installation via homebrew-askr tap, extended cross-repo boundary checks to Bash tool calls, documented real Homebrew installation paths in README.md, rebased parallel work from another agent session, and added 64 new tests for guard_runner.py and pre_tool_use.py achieving 122/122 passing tests.
discussion_summary: Askr has progressed through four major phases: Phase 3 (notifications), Phase 3.5 (permission guard), and Phase 4 (approval workflow) are fully implemented, tested, and documented. The v0.1.0 release cycle was completed with correct tarball sha256, fixed Homebrew formula, and homebrew-askr tap verification. This session rebased and merged parallel work from another agent branch, updated README.md to document real Homebrew install commands (one-liner `brew install BippinShekar/askr/askr` and tap-then-install form with explanation of why bare `brew install askr` requires homebrew-core merge), merged Bash-boundary guard extension work, and integrated comprehensive test coverage for guard subsystem (64 new tests). All 122 tests pass. Two known gaps remain: guard_warning notification type (non-blocking guard warnings) is dead code—never invoked from pre_tool_use.py despite being wired into extension.js—and PreCompact emergency handover still routes through hardcoded boilerplate instead of real LLM handover path. This session focused on social media messaging strategy around Homebrew notability review friction rather than code changes.

accomplishments.what: LinkedIn location combobox field filling fixed with city name extraction and fallback retry pattern
Identified root cause of LinkedIn location field failures: full location strings do not trigger city autocomplete dropdown
Implemented two-part fix: prompt instructs extraction of city name from full location string, with retry on failure
Killed orphaned uvicorn process blocking backend logs
Conducted comprehensive security audit of apply agent code generation paths with four hardening fixes against prompt injection attacks
Fixed resume PDF portfolio URL lookup from qa_bank.portfolio_url to application_prefill.answers.portfolio_url
Updated PDF generator to render 'Portfolio' as link label instead of domain URL
Diagnosed Ramp application failure as Ashby spam_warning state (browser fingerprinting-based anti-bot detection)
Implemented spam_warning recovery with 'Learn more' probe to locate submit/submit-again button
Extended spam_warning handling to distinguish overlay banner (resubmit after scroll) vs form replacement (hard refresh required)
Refactored spam recovery strategy to defer spam-flagged jobs to end of session instead of inline retry
Investigated queue drain architecture and browser_stream replay buffer lifecycle
Implemented permission_gate.py to detect dangerous permissions (skip-permissions, unrestricted Bash, rm in allow list)
Implemented session_start.py to hold queued tasks instead of auto-injecting when dangerous permissions detected
Wired task_approval_pending notification type into Cursor extension.js for IDE popup rendering
Extended cross-repo boundary checks to Bash tool calls in guard_runner.py
Updated README.md to document real Homebrew installation paths (one-liner and tap-then-install forms)
Added 64 new tests for guard_runner.py and pre_tool_use.py achieving 122/122 passing tests

next_actions.action: Fix PreCompact emergency handover to route through real LLM handover path instead of hardcoded boilerplate in checkpoint.py create_checkpoint function (trigger_type==emergency branch)
next_actions.why: Known gap blocking proper emergency task handover between sessions; currently uses placeholder text instead of invoking actual LLM context transfer

next_actions.action: Activate guard_warning notification type dead code path: add invocation from pre_tool_use.py for non-blocking guard warnings and wire through HOOK_MAP to enable IDE popup rendering in extension.js
next_actions.why: guard_warning type is fully implemented in extension.js and notification.json but never triggered from Python guard logic; completing this enables non-blocking guard warning UX

decisions.decision: Skip Homebrew notability review post on social media
decisions.reason: User determined that announcing brew tap/sha256 support signals 'ready to launch' prematurely, breaking the deadpan voice of prior posts and revealing project existence before intended public launch

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Post about Homebrew notability review and stars requirement with engagement-bait framing ('🎉 brew tap now live')
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: brother nobody knows I am building yet, and that tweet makes it look like I am ready to launch and enable brew install

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Closing punchline 'Valid ngl.???' or similar commentary after the three-line joke
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: that sounds like a critique against them, but that is probably the most correct way
